action.reasoning: Observation: val mean_r2 reached 0.586 (q~0.3 inferred) but test r2_q ~-1.57e6 drives mean_r2~-6.8e5 (worse than iter9 best of -23k); t/rad/precip also regressed to 0.50/0.89/0.61. Training ~15min/30 epochs, curves show val_mse plateau ~0.0046. Analysis: Severe gen gap on q-tendency (r2 hypersensitive to outliers/extremes on test dist shift from val year-8); mean_val_r2 selection deprioritizes q, capacity/reg still insufficient despite noise/WD/clip/small-output-init/weighted-loss. Decision: Select best ckpt on val_r2_q to target bottleneck, reduce to hidden=768/blocks=4/dropout=0.4, raise noise=0.1/q_weight=8/WD=1e-3, output_std=5e-5, switch to CosineAnnealingLR, epochs=50; add [-50,50] clip in predict.py to cap extremes; inspect target/input ranges first then train+submit_eval on best.pt (time allows).
action.command:
python -c '
import numpy as np
data_dir = "/data"
print("Data stats:")
val_t = np.load(data_dir + "/val_target.npy")
print("val_t maxabs t:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,:60])))
print("val_t maxabs q:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,60:120])))
print("val_t maxabs rad:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,[120,121,124,125,126,127]])))
print("val_t maxabs precip:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,122:124])))
val_i = np.load(data_dir + "/val_input.npy")
print("val_input maxabs:", np.max(np.abs(val_i)))
print("val_t mean std per group:", np.mean(np.std(val_t[:,:60], axis=0)), np.mean(np.std(val_t[:,60:120], axis=0)))
' && python train.py
